descriptions.AccordionContent: Contains the collapsible content for an item.
descriptions.AccordionHeader: Wraps an AccordionTrigger. Use the asChild prop to update it to the appropriate heading level for your page.
descriptions.AccordionItem: Contains all the parts of a collapsible section.
descriptions.AccordionRoot: Contains all the parts of an Accordion
descriptions.AccordionTrigger: Toggles the collapsed state of its associated item. It should be nested inside of an AccordionHeader.
descriptions.AlertDialogAction: A button that closes the dialog. These buttons should be distinguished visually from the AlertDialogCancel button.
descriptions.AlertDialogCancel: A button that closes the dialog. This button should be distinguished visually from AlertDialogAction buttons.
descriptions.AlertDialogContent: Contains content to be rendered when the dialog is open.
descriptions.AlertDialogDescription: An accessible description to be announced when the dialog is opened. Alternatively, you can provide aria-describedby to AlertDialogContent and exclude this component.
descriptions.AlertDialogOverlay: A layer that covers the inert portion of the view when the dialog is open.
descriptions.AlertDialogPortal: When used, portals your overlay and content parts into the body .
descriptions.AlertDialogRoot: Contains all the parts of an alert dialog.
descriptions.AlertDialogTitle: An accessible name to be announced when the dialog is opened. Alternatively, you can provide aria-label or aria-labelledby to AlertDialogContent and exclude this component.
descriptions.AlertDialogTrigger: A button that opens the dialog.
descriptions.AspectRatio: Contains the content you want to constrain to a given ratio.
descriptions.AutocompleteInput: The input component to search through the autocomplete items. Typing updates the modelValue directly.
descriptions.AutocompleteRoot: Contains all the parts of an Autocomplete.
descriptions.AvatarFallback: An element that renders when the image hasn't loaded. This means whilst it's loading, or if there was an error. If you notice a flash during loading, you can provide a delayMs prop to delay its rendering so it only renders for those with slower connections. For more control, use the @loadingStatusChange emit on AvatarImage.
descriptions.AvatarImage: The image to render. By default it will only render when it has loaded. You can use the @loadingStatusChange handler if you need more control.
descriptions.AvatarRoot: Contains all the parts of an avatar
descriptions.CalendarCell: Container for wrapping the calendar cells.
descriptions.CalendarCellTrigger: Interactable container for displaying the cell dates. Clicking it selects the date.
descriptions.CalendarGrid: Container for wrapping the calendar grid.
descriptions.CalendarGridBody: Container for wrapping the grid body.
descriptions.CalendarGridHead: Container for wrapping the grid head.
descriptions.CalendarGridRow: Container for wrapping the grid row.
descriptions.CalendarHeadCell: Container for wrapping the head cell. Used for displaying the week days.
descriptions.CalendarHeader: Contains the navigation buttons and the heading segments.
descriptions.CalendarHeading: Heading for displaying the current month and year
descriptions.CalendarNext: Calendar navigation button. It navigates the calendar one month/year/decade in the future based on the current calendar view.
descriptions.CalendarPrev: Calendar navigation button. It navigates the calendar one month/year/decade in the past based on the current calendar view.
descriptions.CalendarRoot: Contains all the parts of a calendar
descriptions.CheckboxGroupRoot: Wrapper around CheckboxRoot to support array of modelValue
descriptions.CheckboxIndicator: Renders when the checkbox is in a checked or indeterminate state. You can style this element directly, or you can use it as a wrapper to put an icon into, or both.
descriptions.CheckboxRoot: Contains all the parts of a checkbox. An input will also render when used within a form to ensure events propagate correctly.
descriptions.CollapsibleContent: The component that contains the collapsible content.
descriptions.CollapsibleRoot: Contains all the parts of a collapsible
descriptions.CollapsibleTrigger: The button that toggles the collapsible
descriptions.ColorAreaArea: The interactive area component where users can select color values by clicking or dragging.
descriptions.ColorAreaRoot: The root component that provides the color area context and state management.
descriptions.ColorAreaThumb: The draggable thumb component that indicates the current position in the color area.
descriptions.ColorFieldInput: The input element for entering color values.
descriptions.ColorFieldRoot: The root component that provides the color field context and state management.
descriptions.ColorSliderRoot: The root component that provides the slider functionality and context.
descriptions.ColorSliderThumb: The draggable thumb component for selecting values.
descriptions.ColorSliderTrack: The track component that displays the color gradient background.
descriptions.ColorSwatch: The main component that displays a color swatch.
descriptions.ColorSwatchPickerItem: The item that represents a selectable color swatch.
descriptions.ColorSwatchPickerItemIndicator: The component that indicates the selected color swatch within an item.
descriptions.ColorSwatchPickerItemSwatch: The component that displays the color swatch within an item.
descriptions.ColorSwatchPickerRoot: The main component that displays a color swatch picker.
descriptions.ComboboxAnchor: Used as an anchor if you set ComboboxContent's position to popper.
descriptions.ComboboxArrow: An optional arrow element to render alongside the content. This can be used to help visually link the trigger with the ComboboxContent. Must be rendered inside ComboboxContent. Only available when position is set to popper.
descriptions.ComboboxCancel: The button that clears the search term.
descriptions.ComboboxContent: The component that pops out when the combobox is open.
descriptions.ComboboxEmpty: Shown when none of the items match the query.
descriptions.ComboboxGroup: Used to group multiple items. use in conjunction with ComboboxLabel to ensure good accessibility via automatic labelling.
descriptions.ComboboxInput: The input component to search through the combobox items.
descriptions.ComboboxItem: The component that contains the combobox items.
descriptions.ComboboxItemIndicator: Renders when the item is selected. You can style this element directly, or you can use it as a wrapper to put an icon into, or both.
descriptions.ComboboxLabel: Used to render the label of a group. It won't be focusable using arrow keys.
descriptions.ComboboxPortal: When used, portals the content part into the body. You need to set position="popper" for ComboboxContent to make sure the position was automatically computed similar to Popover or DropdownMenu.
descriptions.ComboboxRoot: Contains all the parts of a Combobox
descriptions.ComboboxSeparator: Used to visually separate items in the Combobox
descriptions.ComboboxTrigger: The button that toggles the Combobox Content.
descriptions.ComboboxViewport: The scrolling viewport that contains all of the items.
descriptions.ComboboxVirtualizer: Virtual container to achieve list virtualization. Combobox items **must** be filtered manually before passing them over to the virtualizer. See example below. See the virtualization guide for more general info on virtualization.
descriptions.ContextMenuArrow: An optional arrow element to render alongside a submenu. This can be used to help visually link the trigger item with the ContextMenu.Content. Must be rendered inside ContextMenu.Content.
descriptions.ContextMenuCheckboxItem: An item that can be controlled and rendered like a checkbox.
descriptions.ContextMenuContent: The component that pops out in an open context menu.
descriptions.ContextMenuItem: The component that contains the context menu items.
descriptions.ContextMenuItemIndicator: Renders when the parent ContextMenu.CheckboxItem or ContextMenu.RadioItem is checked. You can style this element directly, or you can use it as a wrapper to put an icon into, or both.
descriptions.ContextMenuLabel: Used to render a label. It won't be focusable using arrow keys.
descriptions.ContextMenuPortal: When used, portals the content part into the body.
descriptions.ContextMenuRadioItem: An item that can be controlled and rendered like a radio.
descriptions.ContextMenuRoot: Contains all the parts of a context menu.
descriptions.ContextMenuSeparator: Used to visually separate items in the context menu.
descriptions.ContextMenuSub: Contains all the parts of a submenu.
descriptions.ContextMenuSubContent: The component that pops out when a submenu is open. Must be rendered inside ContextMenu.Sub.
descriptions.ContextMenuSubTrigger: An item that opens a submenu. Must be rendered inside ContextMenu.Sub.
descriptions.ContextMenuTrigger: The area that opens the context menu. Wrap it around the target you want the context menu to open from when right-clicking (or using the relevant keyboard shortcuts).
descriptions.DateFieldRoot: Contains all the parts of a date field
descriptions.DatePickerAnchor: An optional element to position the DatePickerContent against. If this part is not used, the content will position alongside the DatePickerTrigger.
descriptions.DatePickerArrow: An optional arrow element to render alongside the popover. This can be used to help visually link the anchor with the DatePickerContent. Must be rendered inside DatePickerContent.
descriptions.DatePickerCalendar: Contains all the parts of a calendar
descriptions.DatePickerCell: Container for wrapping the calendar cells.
descriptions.DatePickerCellTrigger: Interactable container for displaying the cell dates. Clicking it selects the date.
descriptions.DatePickerClose: The button that closes an open date picker.
descriptions.DatePickerContent: The component that pops out when the popover is open.
descriptions.DatePickerField: Contains the date picker date field segments and trigger
descriptions.DatePickerGrid: Container for wrapping the calendar grid.
descriptions.DatePickerGridBody: Container for wrapping the grid body.
descriptions.DatePickerGridHead: Container for wrapping the grid head.
descriptions.DatePickerGridRow: Container for wrapping the grid row.
descriptions.DatePickerHeadCell: Container for wrapping the head cell. Used for displaying the week days.
descriptions.DatePickerHeader: Contains the navigation buttons and the heading segments.
descriptions.DatePickerHeading: Heading for displaying the current month and year/
descriptions.DatePickerInput: Contains the date picker date field segments
descriptions.DatePickerNext: Calendar navigation button. It navigates the calendar one month/year/decade in the future based on the current calendar view.
descriptions.DatePickerPrev: Calendar navigation button. It navigates the calendar one month/year/decade in the past based on the current calendar view.
descriptions.DatePickerRoot: Contains all the parts of a date picker
descriptions.DatePickerTrigger: The button that toggles the popover. By default, the DatePickerContent will position itself against the trigger.
descriptions.DateRangeFieldRoot: Contains all the parts of a date field
descriptions.DateRangePickerAnchor: An optional element to position the DateRangePickerContent against. If this part is not used, the content will position alongside the DateRangePickerTrigger.
descriptions.DateRangePickerArrow: An optional arrow element to render alongside the popover. This can be used to help visually link the anchor with the DateRangePickerContent. Must be rendered inside DateRangePickerContent.
descriptions.DateRangePickerCalendar: Contains all the parts of a calendar
descriptions.DateRangePickerCell: Container for wrapping the calendar cells.
descriptions.DateRangePickerCellTrigger: Interactable container for displaying the cell dates. Clicking it selects the date.
descriptions.DateRangePickerClose: The button that closes an open date picker.
descriptions.DateRangePickerContent: The component that pops out when the popover is open.
descriptions.DateRangePickerField: Contains the date picker date field segments and trigger
descriptions.DateRangePickerGrid: Container for wrapping the calendar grid.
descriptions.DateRangePickerGridBody: Container for wrapping the grid body.
descriptions.DateRangePickerGridHead: Container for wrapping the grid head.
descriptions.DateRangePickerGridRow: Container for wrapping the grid row.
descriptions.DateRangePickerHeadCell: Container for wrapping the head cell. Used for displaying the week days.
descriptions.DateRangePickerHeader: Contains the navigation buttons and the heading segments.
descriptions.DateRangePickerHeading: Heading for displaying the current month and year
descriptions.DateRangePickerInput: Contains the date picker date field segments
descriptions.DateRangePickerNext: Calendar navigation button. It navigates the calendar one month/year/decade in the future based on the current calendar view.
descriptions.DateRangePickerPrev: Calendar navigation button. It navigates the calendar one month/year/decade in the past based on the current calendar view.
descriptions.DateRangePickerRoot: Contains all the parts of a date picker
descriptions.DateRangePickerTrigger: The button that toggles the popover. By default, the DateRangePickerContent will position itself against the trigger.
descriptions.DialogClose: The button that closes the dialog
descriptions.DialogContent: Contains content to be rendered in the open dialog
descriptions.DialogDescription: An optional accessible description to be announced when the dialog is opened. If you want to hide the description, wrap it inside our Visually Hidden utility like this . If you want to remove the description entirely, remove this part and pass :aria-describedby="undefined" to DialogContent.
descriptions.DialogOverlay: A layer that covers the inert portion of the view when the dialog is open.
descriptions.DialogPortal: When used, portals your overlay and content parts into the body.
descriptions.DialogRoot: Contains all the parts of a dialog
descriptions.DialogTitle: An accessible title to be announced when the dialog is opened. If you want to hide the title, wrap it inside our Visually Hidden utility like this .
descriptions.DialogTrigger: The button that opens the dialog
descriptions.DrawerClose: The button that closes the drawer.
descriptions.DrawerContent: Contains the content to be rendered in the open drawer. Owns the swipe gesture and exposes the drag offset through CSS custom properties (see Animating the drawer). Also aliased as DrawerPopup for Base UI parity.
descriptions.DrawerDescription: An optional accessible description to be announced when the drawer is opened. If you want to hide the description, wrap it inside our Visually Hidden utility like this . If you want to remove the description entirely, remove this part and pass :aria-describedby="undefined" to DrawerContent.
descriptions.DrawerHandle: A visual grab handle that hints the drawer can be dragged. It is purely decorative (aria-hidden) — the whole content is draggable regardless.
descriptions.DrawerIndent: Wraps page content that should visually shift (scale/indent) as the drawer is swiped, mimicking the native iOS "card stack" effect. Reads the visual state from a parent DrawerProvider and syncs the --drawer-swipe-progress and --drawer-height CSS variables onto its element.
descriptions.DrawerIndentBackground: The backdrop layer revealed behind an indented page (typically a solid colour that peeks out as the page scales down). Companion to Indent.
descriptions.DrawerOverlay: A layer that covers the inert portion of the view when the drawer is open. Only rendered when the drawer is modal.
descriptions.DrawerPortal: When used, portals your overlay and content parts into the body.
descriptions.DrawerRoot: Contains all the parts of a drawer. Manages open state, modality, swipe direction and snap points, and provides the context consumed by every other part.
descriptions.DrawerSwipeArea: An off-screen edge area that lets users swipe the drawer **open**. By default it listens on the opposite side of the Root's swipeDirection.
descriptions.DrawerTitle: An accessible title to be announced when the drawer is opened. If you want to hide the title, wrap it inside our Visually Hidden utility like this .
descriptions.DrawerTrigger: The button that opens the drawer.
descriptions.DrawerViewport: An optional scrollable wrapper for the drawer content. Mirrors Base UI's Drawer.Viewport and carries a data-drawer-viewport attribute for downstream selectors.
descriptions.DropdownMenuArrow: An optional arrow element to render alongside the dropdown menu. This can be used to help visually link the trigger with the DropdownMenuContent. Must be rendered inside DropdownMenuContent.
descriptions.DropdownMenuCheckboxItem: An item that can be controlled and rendered like a checkbox.
descriptions.DropdownMenuContent: The component that pops out when the dropdown menu is open.
descriptions.DropdownMenuItem: The component that contains the dropdown menu items.
descriptions.DropdownMenuItemIndicator: Renders when the parent DropdownMenuCheckboxItem or DropdownMenuRadioItem is checked. You can style this element directly, or you can use it as a wrapper to put an icon into, or both.
descriptions.DropdownMenuLabel: Used to render a label. It won't be focusable using arrow keys.
descriptions.DropdownMenuPortal: When used, portals the content part into the body.
descriptions.DropdownMenuRadioItem: An item that can be controlled and rendered like a radio.
descriptions.DropdownMenuRoot: Contains all the parts of a dropdown menu.
descriptions.DropdownMenuSeparator: Used to visually separate items in the dropdown menu.
descriptions.DropdownMenuSub: Contains all the parts of a submenu.
descriptions.DropdownMenuSubContent: The component that pops out when a submenu is open. Must be rendered inside DropdownMenuSub.
descriptions.DropdownMenuSubTrigger: An item that opens a submenu. Must be rendered inside DropdownMenuSub.
descriptions.DropdownMenuTrigger: The button that toggles the dropdown menu. By default, the DropdownMenuContent will position itself against the trigger.
descriptions.EditableArea: Contains the text parts of an editable component.
descriptions.EditableCancelTrigger: Contains the cancel trigger of the editable component.
descriptions.EditableEditTrigger: Contains the edit trigger of the editable component.
descriptions.EditableInput: Contains the input of an editable component.
descriptions.EditablePreview: Contains the preview of the editable component.
descriptions.EditableRoot: Contains all the parts of an editable component.
descriptions.EditableSubmitTrigger: Contains the submit trigger of the editable component.
descriptions.HoverCardArrow: An optional arrow element to render alongside the hover card. This can be used to help visually link the trigger with the HoverCardContent. Must be rendered inside HoverCardContent.
descriptions.HoverCardContent: The component that pops out when the hover card is open.
descriptions.HoverCardPortal: When used, portals the content part into the body.
descriptions.HoverCardRoot: Contains all the parts of a hover card.
descriptions.HoverCardTrigger: The link that opens the hover card when hovered.
descriptions.Label: Contains the content for the label.
descriptions.ListboxContent: Contains all the listbox group and items.
descriptions.ListboxGroup: Used to group multiple items. use in conjunction with ListboxGroupLabel to ensure good accessibility via automatic labelling.
descriptions.ListboxGroupLabel: Used to render the label of a group. It won't be focusable using arrow keys.
descriptions.ListboxItemIndicator: Renders when the item is selected. You can style this element directly, or you can use it as a wrapper to put an icon into, or both.
descriptions.ListboxRoot: Contains all the parts of a listbox. An input will also render when used within a form to ensure events propagate correctly.
descriptions.ListboxVirtualizer: Virtual container to achieve list virtualization.
descriptions.MenubarArrow: An optional arrow element to render alongside a menubar menu. This can be used to help visually link the trigger with the MenubarContent. Must be rendered inside MenubarContent.
descriptions.MenubarCheckboxItem: An item that can be controlled and rendered like a checkbox.
descriptions.MenubarContent: The component that pops out when a menu is open.
descriptions.MenubarItem: The component that contains the menubar items.
descriptions.MenubarItemIndicator: Renders when the parent MenubarCheckboxItem or MenubarRadioItem is checked. You can style this element directly, or you can use it as a wrapper to put an icon into, or both.
descriptions.MenubarLabel: Used to render a label. It won't be focusable using arrow keys.
descriptions.MenubarMenu: A top level menu item, contains a trigger with content combination.
descriptions.MenubarPortal: When used, portals the content part into the body.
descriptions.MenubarRadioItem: An item that can be controlled and rendered like a radio.
descriptions.MenubarRoot: Contains all the parts of a menubar
descriptions.MenubarSeparator: Used to visually separate items in a menubar menu.
descriptions.MenubarSub: Contains all the parts of a submenu.
descriptions.MenubarSubContent: The component that pops out when a submenu is open. Must be rendered inside MenubarSub.
descriptions.MenubarSubTrigger: An item that opens a submenu. Must be rendered inside MenubarSub.
descriptions.MenubarTrigger: The button that toggles the content. By default, the MenubarContent will position itself against the trigger.
descriptions.MonthPickerCell: Container for wrapping the month picker cells.
descriptions.MonthPickerCellTrigger: Interactable container for displaying the cell months. Clicking it selects the month.
descriptions.MonthPickerGrid: Container for wrapping the month picker grid.
descriptions.MonthPickerGridBody: Container for wrapping the grid body.
descriptions.MonthPickerGridRow: Container for wrapping the grid row.
descriptions.MonthPickerHeader: Contains the navigation buttons and the heading segments.
descriptions.MonthPickerHeading: Heading for displaying the current year.
descriptions.MonthPickerNext: Calendar navigation button. It navigates the calendar one year in the future.
descriptions.MonthPickerPrev: Calendar navigation button. It navigates the calendar one year in the past.
descriptions.MonthPickerRoot: Contains all the parts of a month picker
descriptions.MonthRangePickerCell: Container for wrapping the month range picker cells.
descriptions.MonthRangePickerCellTrigger: Interactable container for displaying the cell months. Clicking it selects the month.
descriptions.MonthRangePickerGrid: Container for wrapping the month range picker grid.
descriptions.MonthRangePickerGridBody: Container for wrapping the grid body.
descriptions.MonthRangePickerGridRow: Container for wrapping the grid row.
descriptions.MonthRangePickerHeader: Contains the navigation buttons and the heading segments.
descriptions.MonthRangePickerHeading: Heading for displaying the current year.
descriptions.MonthRangePickerNext: Calendar navigation button. It navigates the calendar one year in the future.
descriptions.MonthRangePickerPrev: Calendar navigation button. It navigates the calendar one year in the past.
descriptions.MonthRangePickerRoot: Contains all the parts of a month range picker
descriptions.NavigationMenuContent: Contains the content associated with each trigger.
descriptions.NavigationMenuIndicator: An optional indicator element that renders below the list, is used to highlight the currently active trigger.
descriptions.NavigationMenuItem: A top level menu item, contains a link or trigger with content combination.
descriptions.NavigationMenuList: Contains the top level menu items.
descriptions.NavigationMenuRoot: Contains all the parts of a navigation menu.
descriptions.NavigationMenuSub: Signifies a submenu. Use it in place of the root part when nested to create a submenu.
descriptions.NavigationMenuTrigger: The button that toggles the content.
descriptions.NavigationMenuViewport: An optional viewport element that is used to render active content outside of the list.
descriptions.NumberFieldDecrement: The button that decreases the value.
descriptions.NumberFieldIncrement: The button that increases the value.
descriptions.NumberFieldInput: Input The input component that renders the text value based on value and format options.
descriptions.NumberFieldRoot: Contains all the parts of a number field. An input will also render when used within a form to ensure events propagate correctly.
descriptions.PaginationEllipsis: Placeholder element when the list is long, and only a small amount of siblingCount was set and showEdges was set to true.
descriptions.PaginationFirst: Triggers that set the page value to 1
descriptions.PaginationItem: Used to render the button that changes the current page.
descriptions.PaginationLast: Triggers that set the page value to the last page
descriptions.PaginationList: Used to show the list of pages. It also makes pagination accessible to assistive technologies.
descriptions.PaginationNext: Triggers that set the page value to the next page
descriptions.PaginationPrev: Triggers that set the page value to the previous page
descriptions.PaginationRoot: Contains all of the paginations parts.
descriptions.PinInputInput: Input field for Pin Input. You can add as many input as you like.
descriptions.PinInputRoot: Contains all the parts of a pin. An input will also render when used within a form to ensure events propagate correctly.
descriptions.PopoverAnchor: An optional element to position the PopoverContent against. If this part is not used, the content will position alongside the PopoverTrigger .
descriptions.PopoverArrow: An optional arrow element to render alongside the popover. This can be used to help visually link the anchor with the PopoverContent. Must be rendered inside PopoverContent.
descriptions.PopoverClose: The button that closes an open popover.
descriptions.PopoverContent: The component that pops out when the popover is open.
descriptions.PopoverPortal: When used, portals the content part into the body.
descriptions.PopoverRoot: Contains all the parts of a popover.
descriptions.PopoverTrigger: The button that toggles the popover. By default, the PopoverContent will position itself against the trigger.
descriptions.ProgressIndicator: Used to show the progress visually. It also makes progress accessible to assistive technologies.
descriptions.ProgressRoot: Contains all of the progress parts.
descriptions.RadioGroupIndicator: Renders when the radio item is in a checked state. You can style this element directly, or you can use it as a wrapper to put an icon into, or both.
descriptions.RadioGroupItem: An item in the group that can be checked. An input will also render when used within a form to ensure events propagate correctly.
descriptions.RadioGroupRoot: Contains all the parts of a radio group.
descriptions.RangeCalendarCell: Container for wrapping the calendar cells.
descriptions.RangeCalendarCellTrigger: Interactable container for displaying the cell dates. Clicking it selects the date.
descriptions.RangeCalendarGrid: Container for wrapping the calendar grid.
descriptions.RangeCalendarGridBody: Container for wrapping the grid body.
descriptions.RangeCalendarGridHead: Container for wrapping the grid head.
descriptions.RangeCalendarGridRow: Container for wrapping the grid row.
descriptions.RangeCalendarHeadCell: Container for wrapping the head cell. Used for displaying the week days.
descriptions.RangeCalendarHeader: Contains the navigation buttons and the heading segments.
descriptions.RangeCalendarHeading: Heading for displaying the current month and year.
descriptions.RangeCalendarNext: Calendar navigation button. It navigates the calendar one month/year/decade in the future based on the current calendar view.
descriptions.RangeCalendarPrev: Calendar navigation button. It navigates the calendar one month/year/decade in the past based on the current calendar view.
descriptions.RangeCalendarRoot: Contains all the parts of a calendar
descriptions.RatingItem: Wraps a single rating value (e.g. a single star). It computes the list of steps that compose this item based on the root's step prop, exposed via the default slot. Renders a label by default.
descriptions.RatingItemIndicator: The interactive indicator rendered for each step of an item. It reflects whether the step is active based on the current (or hovered) value.
descriptions.RatingRoot: Contains all the parts of a rating and provides the rating state. Renders a Radio Group under the hood, so it supports form submission and keyboard navigation.
descriptions.ScrollAreaCorner: The corner where both vertical and horizontal scrollbars meet.
descriptions.ScrollAreaRoot: Contains all the parts of a scroll area.
descriptions.ScrollAreaScrollbar: The vertical scrollbar. Add a second Scrollbar with an orientation prop to enable horizontal scrolling.
descriptions.ScrollAreaThumb: The thumb to be used in ScrollAreaScrollbar.
descriptions.ScrollAreaViewport: The viewport area of the scroll area.
descriptions.SelectArrow: An optional arrow element to render alongside the content. This can be used to help visually link the trigger with the SelectContent. Must be rendered inside SelectContent. Only available when position is set to popper.
descriptions.SelectContent: The component that pops out when the select is open.
descriptions.SelectGroup: Used to group multiple items. use in conjunction with SelectLabel to ensure good accessibility via automatic labelling.
descriptions.SelectItem: The component that contains the select items.
descriptions.SelectItemIndicator: Renders when the item is selected. You can style this element directly, or you can use it as a wrapper to put an icon into, or both.
descriptions.SelectItemText: The textual part of the item. It should only contain the text you want to see in the trigger when that item is selected. It should not be styled to ensure correct positioning.
descriptions.SelectLabel: Used to render the label of a group. It won't be focusable using arrow keys.
descriptions.SelectPortal: When used, portals the content part into the body.
descriptions.SelectRoot: Contains all the parts of a Select
descriptions.SelectScrollDownButton: An optional button used as an affordance to show the viewport overflow as well as functionally enable scrolling downwards.
descriptions.SelectScrollUpButton: An optional button used as an affordance to show the viewport overflow as well as functionally enable scrolling upwards.
descriptions.SelectSeparator: Used to visually separate items in the Select
descriptions.SelectTrigger: The button that toggles the Select The SelectContent will position itself by aligning over the trigger.
descriptions.SelectValue: The part that reflects the selected value. By default the selected item's text will be rendered. if you require more control, you can instead control the select and pass your own children. It should not be styled to ensure correct positioning. An optional placeholder prop is also available for when the select has no value.
descriptions.SelectViewport: The scrolling viewport that contains all of the items.
descriptions.SliderRange: The range part. Must live inside SliderTrack.
descriptions.SliderRoot: Contains all the parts of a slider. It will render an input for each thumb when used within a form to ensure events propagate correctly.
descriptions.SliderThumb: A draggable thumb. You can render multiple thumbs.
descriptions.SliderTrack: The track that contains the SliderRange.
descriptions.SplitterGroup: Contains all the parts of a Splitter.
descriptions.StepperItem: An optional accessible description to be announced when the stepper trigger is focused. If you want to hide the description, wrap it inside our Visually Hidden utility like this . If you want to remove the description entirely, remove this part and pass aria-describedby="undefined" to StepperTrigger.
descriptions.StepperRoot: Contains all the stepper component parts.
descriptions.StepperTitle: An accessible title to be announced when the stepper trigger is focused. If you want to hide the title, wrap it inside our Visually Hidden utility like this .
descriptions.StepperTrigger: The trigger that toggles the step.
descriptions.SwitchRoot: Contains all the parts of a switch. An input will also render when used within a form to ensure events propagate correctly.
descriptions.SwitchThumb: The thumb that is used to visually indicate whether the switch is on or off.
descriptions.TabsContent: Contains the content associated with each trigger.
descriptions.TabsIndicator: The indicator that highlights the current active tab.
descriptions.TabsList: Contains the triggers that are aligned along the edge of the active content.
descriptions.TabsRoot: Contains all the tabs component parts.
descriptions.TabsTrigger: The button that activates its associated content.
descriptions.TagsInputClear: The button that remove all tags.
descriptions.TagsInputInput: The input element for the tags input.
descriptions.TagsInputItem: The component that contains the tag.
descriptions.TagsInputItemDelete: The button that delete the associate tag.
descriptions.TagsInputItemText: The textual part of the tag. Important for accessibility.
descriptions.TagsInputRoot: Contains all the tags input component parts.
descriptions.TimeFieldRoot: Contains all the parts of a time field
descriptions.TimeRangeFieldRoot: Contains all the parts of a time range field.
descriptions.ToastAction: An action that is safe to ignore to ensure users are not expected to complete tasks with unexpected side effects as a result of a time limit. When obtaining a user response is necessary, portal an "AlertDialog" styled as a toast into the viewport instead.
descriptions.ToastClose: A button that allows users to dismiss the toast before its duration has elapsed.
descriptions.ToastPortal: When used, portals the content part into the body.
descriptions.ToastProvider: The provider that wraps your toasts and toast viewport. It usually wraps the application.
descriptions.ToastRoot: The toast that automatically closes. It should not be held open to acquire a user response.
descriptions.ToastTitle: An optional title for the toast
descriptions.ToastViewport: The fixed area where toasts appear. Users can jump to the viewport by pressing a hotkey. It is up to you to ensure the discoverability of the hotkey for keyboard users.
descriptions.ToggleGroupRoot: Contains all the parts of a toggle group.
descriptions.ToolbarRoot: Contains all the toolbar component parts.
descriptions.ToolbarSeparator: Used to visually separate items in the toolbar
descriptions.ToolbarToggleGroup: A set of two-state buttons that can be toggled on or off.
descriptions.TooltipArrow: An optional arrow element to render alongside the tooltip. This can be used to help visually link the trigger with the TooltipContent. Must be rendered inside TooltipContent.
descriptions.TooltipContent: The component that pops out when the tooltip is open.
descriptions.TooltipPortal: When used, portals the content part into the body.
descriptions.TooltipProvider: Wraps your app to provide global functionality to your tooltips.
descriptions.TooltipRoot: Contains all the parts of a tooltip.
descriptions.TooltipTrigger: The button that toggles the tooltip. By default, the TooltipContent will position itself against the trigger.
descriptions.TreeRoot: Contains all the parts of a tree.
descriptions.TreeVirtualizer: Virtual container to achieve list virtualization.
descriptions.YearPickerCell: Container for wrapping the year picker cells.
descriptions.YearPickerCellTrigger: Interactable container for displaying the cell years. Clicking it selects the year.
descriptions.YearPickerGrid: Container for wrapping the year picker grid.
descriptions.YearPickerGridBody: Container for wrapping the grid body.
descriptions.YearPickerGridRow: Container for wrapping the grid row.
descriptions.YearPickerHeader: Contains the navigation buttons and the heading segments.
descriptions.YearPickerHeading: Heading for displaying the current year range (e.g., "2020 - 2031").
descriptions.YearPickerNext: Calendar navigation button. It navigates the calendar one page (default: 12 years) in the future.
descriptions.YearPickerPrev: Calendar navigation button. It navigates the calendar one page (default: 12 years) in the past.
descriptions.YearPickerRoot: Contains all the parts of a year picker
descriptions.YearRangePickerCell: Container for wrapping the year range picker cells.
descriptions.YearRangePickerCellTrigger: Interactable container for displaying the cell years. Clicking it selects the year.
descriptions.YearRangePickerGrid: Container for wrapping the year range picker grid.
descriptions.YearRangePickerGridBody: Container for wrapping the grid body.
descriptions.YearRangePickerGridRow: Container for wrapping the grid row.
descriptions.YearRangePickerHeader: Contains the navigation buttons and the heading segments.
descriptions.YearRangePickerHeading: Heading for displaying the current year range.
descriptions.YearRangePickerNext: Calendar navigation button. It navigates the calendar one page (12 years by default) in the future.
descriptions.YearRangePickerPrev: Calendar navigation button. It navigates the calendar one page (12 years by default) in the past.
descriptions.YearRangePickerRoot: Contains all the parts of a year range picker
